description: Operate and manage a self-hosted GEOrank instance through its HTTP API. Use when asked to log in, inspect a GEOrank account, submit or manage companies, run or read GEO diagnostics, manage solution conversations, expand keywords, inspect usage, or perform authenticated GEOrank administrator work across users, content, experts, providers, modules, homepage releases, and settings. Login guidance defaults to an ordinary user and unlocks administrator actions only after /api/auth/me returns role=admin. Do not use for general GEO strategy, article writing, SEO advice, or GEOrank code development that does not operate a running instance.
systems_doctrine: Structure drives behavior: improve the boundary, feedback loops, drift watch, and leverage points before adding weight.

boundary_map.owned_job: Safely operate a running self-hosted GEOrank instance for ordinary users and verified administrators through one reusable skill.
input_boundary: password supplied through a hidden prompt or named environment variable
company, report, conversation, user, content, or release identifier
boundary_map.output_boundary: A redacted execution receipt containing detected access level, operation, target, dry-run or executed status, API status, resource identifiers, request ID, next safe step, and rollback guidance when applicable.
constraints: passwords and tokens stay out of argv and receipts
administrator actions require a live /api/auth/me role check
destructive actions use an exact canonical path-and-query confirmation phrase
request, response, and session sizes are bounded
session replacement and removal require a recognized GEOrank format marker
uncertain write state requires a confirming read before retry
standards: cross-target packaging and isolated install verification
human_judgment_boundary: Ask one focused clarification when the real job, output, or exclusion boundary is unclear.
Escalate visible tradeoffs when benchmark patterns conflict with local privacy, naming, or governance constraints.
Do not silently broaden the skill into adjacent jobs just because the examples are nearby.

feedback_loops.name: Intent boundary loop
feedback_loops.response: Ask only the highest-leverage clarification before adding package weight.

feedback_loops.name: Reference synthesis loop
feedback_loops.signal: Benchmark patterns are useful only after they are abstracted into borrow and avoid guidance.
feedback_loops.response: Borrow one pattern at a time and keep the rest as reviewer-visible evidence.

feedback_loops.name: Output quality loop
feedback_loops.signal: Generated output may fail in recurring domain-specific ways.
feedback_loops.response: Apply predicted output-risk families as self-repair checks before final output.

feedback_loops.name: Reviewer feedback loop
feedback_loops.signal: Human review catches drift that static checks miss.
feedback_loops.response: Capture lightweight feedback and turn repeated findings into gates or references.

feedback_loops.name: Lifecycle loop
feedback_loops.signal: As reuse grows, the skill needs stronger gates, ownership, and regression evidence.
feedback_loops.response: Promote only when the next gate improves reliability more than context cost.

drift_watch.name: Trigger drift
drift_watch.watch_signal: Users start invoking the skill for adjacent one-off or explanation-only requests.
drift_watch.countermeasure: Add near-neighbor exclusions and route evals before expanding workflow steps.

drift_watch.name: Output drift
drift_watch.watch_signal: Outputs remain valid but become generic, cluttered, or weakly aligned with the user's domain.
drift_watch.countermeasure: Refresh output-risk and artifact-design profiles, then add one self-repair check.

drift_watch.name: Reference drift
drift_watch.watch_signal: Borrowed benchmark patterns no longer fit the local job or add ceremony without payoff.
drift_watch.countermeasure: Re-run reference synthesis and keep only patterns that improve the current boundary.
drift_watch.cadence: per material benchmark or product assumption change

drift_watch.name: Governance drift
drift_watch.watch_signal: Skill usage becomes team-critical while ownership, review cadence, or rollback evidence stays informal.
drift_watch.countermeasure: Promote maturity tier and add reviewer-visible lifecycle evidence.

failure_pattern_map.symptom: The skill handles nearby requests that were never part of the recurring job.
failure_pattern_map.repair: Narrow the description and add explicit non-goals before adding more execution steps.

failure_pattern_map.symptom: The skill has rules but no signal telling authors which rule should change after use.
failure_pattern_map.repair: Turn repeated reviewer feedback into one eval, one reference note, or one self-repair check.

failure_pattern_map.symptom: The result is structurally correct but generic, cluttered, or weakly matched to the user's domain.
failure_pattern_map.repair: Use output-risk families as pre-final checks.

failure_pattern_map.symptom: The role, task, and format are copied from a prompt instead of becoming stable skill behavior.
failure_pattern_map.repair: Convert reusable role/task/format assumptions into workflow, reports, or references.

leverage_points.why: The description is the highest-leverage routing surface.
leverage_points.move: Name the recurring job, expected input, output, and strongest non-goal in compact language.

leverage_points.why: The likely failure families are: Code and command safety, Tone and specificity, Markdown readability.
leverage_points.move: Add only the checks that prevent recurring output mistakes.

leverage_points.why: Team-reused skills need visible ownership, review cadence, and regression evidence.
leverage_points.move: Keep manifest, review viewer, and iteration directions aligned after each material change.

reviewer_rule: Reviewer should ask whether the skill's structure will keep producing the desired behavior after repeated real use.
